Ad Text:
About Cbus
Created by workers, for workers, Cbus Super is one of Australia’s most successful Superannuation funds. For over four decades we’ve proudly represented those who help shape Australia, hard-working individuals who deserve to make the most of their retirement, no matter the industry. As an award-winning fund we’ve been recognised for the benefits provided to our members and our innovative investment approach.  All while staying committed to our member first ethos as a proud Industry Fund.

About the role
The Quantitative Investments Team is responsible for researching and deploying investment strategies and providing quantitative support to Cbus’ Investment Team.
 
The Senior Quantitative Analyst is a key resource in driving continuous research and development activities on internal quantitative strategies, with the aim of improving expected returns and risk characteristics for our fund members.

The role works collaboratively within the Quant team to conduct high-quality quantitative investments research and with a strong focus on alpha generation and risk management.  The role will also communicate research results effectively within the team and with the broader investment team including senior stakeholders.  YOU WILL NEED THE FOLLOWING ATTRIBUTES;


Passion to solve problems with hands-on quantitative investment research.
Track record in idea generation and conducting high-quality quantitative investment research in systematic investment processes with weekly/monthly/quarterly time horizons (high-capacity strategies).
Experience with writing well-structured research code in Python (preferred) or in a similar language.

In addition to industry experience, a tertiary qualification (or higher) in quantitative finance or in a quantitative subject such as mathematics, statistics, engineering, physical sciences, or computer science is well regarded.


Belong at Cbus
We value difference, and embrace people with diverse backgrounds, experiences, gender identities, abilities and thinking styles. We believe that, with diversity of perspectives and experiences, you get better teams and outcomes. We’re looking for people of all genders, races, nationalities, orientations and of all abilities to join us.
  
We’re keen to hear from you
If you've read through the requirements of this role and you feel like you haven’t fully met the criteria, we would still encourage you to apply. We're aware of accessibility barriers when it comes to applying for a job and we want to help. If you require assistance with your application, please contact our Talent Acquisition Team via careers@cbussuper.com.au 

Like to know more about working with Cbus? Listen to some of our videos with members of the Cbus team on our website, cbussuper.com.au/careers
  
Applications Close: 17 March (interviews may commence prior)
This is a full time role based in Melbourne or Sydney.
  
Agencies, please note: All Cbus vacancies are managed by our internal Talent Acquisition Team.  Should external assistance be required we will reach out to our preferred agency partners, Thank you.

Position Description: Senior Technical Business Analyst
 
Focus: Data Integrity & Duplicate Mitigation
Term: 3-Month Contract
Role Purpose
 
This strategic initiative aims to reduce duplicate member records across core systems by enhancing front-end validation logic and improving data capture at the point of entry. As a Senior Technical Business Analyst, you will lead the design and analysis phase, working closely with Data Strategy, Analytics, and Solution Architects to map impacts and define technical requirements for superior identity management.
Key Responsibilities

Workshop Facilitation: Lead sessions with data, architecture, and digital teams to identify and assess duplicate creation pathways.
Impact Analysis: Evaluate the downstream effects of making email and mobile fields mandatory across systems, integrations, and customer-facing processes.
Data Assessment: Conduct detailed reviews of existing non-unique records to provide remediation options and future-state handling logic.
Technical Documentation: Produce high-quality "as-is" vs "to-be" process maps, data flows, validation rules, and decision logs.
Requirement Delivery: Translate analytical findings into functional/technical requirements and user stories within Jira/Confluence.
Strategic Alignment: Partner with Architects to ensure validation changes align with broader solution designs and long-term data strategy.
Skills & Experience Required

Technical BA Expertise: 5+ years of experience in data-driven, integration-heavy, or complex digital enterprise environments.
Data Modeling & Validation: Strong experience defining validation rules and working with large-scale customer/member data models.
Analytical Translation: Demonstrated ability to interpret duplication patterns and translate insights into actionable technical specs.
Tool Proficiency: Advanced skills in Jira, Confluence, and process modeling tools (e.g., Visio, LucidChart, or draw.io).
Stakeholder Influence: Exceptional communication skills with the ability to challenge and align diverse technical and operational groups.
Industry Context: Experience within membership-based organizations, insurance, or financial services is highly regarded.

Permanent Opportunity
Transform complex Finance challenges into high-priority delivery plans by bridging the gap between business strategy and technical execution.
Leverage hands-on Innovation to deliver immediate, tangible value.




We are Woolworths Group

We are Woolworths Group. 200,000+ bright minds, passionate hearts and unique perspectives connected by a shared Purpose – ‘to create better experiences together for a better tomorrow.’ It’s that Purpose that fuels our ambition to explore new ideas, make brave commitments and innovate better ways to meet the food and everyday needs of more than 24 million customers every week. 

If you’re excited to turn today’s blue sky thinking into a better tomorrow for future generations, you’ll find yourself supported and enriched in a dynamic, inclusive and empowering workplace that reflects the diverse communities we serve. With a culture of genuine care, a flexible approach to work and opportunities across the group to grow your career and make a meaningful impact, the possibilities for what we can achieve together are endless.




The Opportunity

As a Senior Technical Business Analyst, you will play a key role in bridging the gap between business needs and technical delivery. Your primary focus will be bringing concepts to life; you will take ambiguous business problems and turn them into prioritised, outcome-focused delivery plans; developing "Proofs of Concept" (PoCs), writing code, and leveraging modern tools to deliver tangible value to the Finance team.




What you’ll do

Stakeholder Partnering: Collaborate closely with Finance teams to identify operational pain points and translate complex business needs into clear, measurable outcomes.
Solution Design: Bridge the gap between business requirements and technical execution by defining success criteria and high-quality project documentation.
Rapid Prototyping: Develop "Proofs of Concept" (POCs) to test hypotheses and demonstrate value quickly before moving to full-scale deployment.
Technical Execution: Build robust solutions using SQL, Python, and Visualisation tools, supporting Data Engineering and Data Science workstreams.
Tool Optimisation: Evaluate and select the most effective tools for the task, ranging from automated scripts and dashboards to advanced AI workflows.
User Validation: Lead business testing and sign-off processes to ensure all technical solutions are trusted, understood, and fit for purpose.




What you’ll bring

Proven Track Record: Experience in a Business Analyst or Technical Product role, specifically within data, analytics, or platform-focused teams.
Stakeholder Translation: Ability to bridge the gap between non-technical stakeholders and technical delivery teams, turning ambiguous pain points into delivery-ready requirements.
Analytical Problem-Solving: A structured approach to troubleshooting and root-cause analysis to engineer effective, business-led solutions.
Domain Expertise: A background in Finance, Commercial Analytics, or Operations, with the ability to navigate complex financial data environments.
Technical Literacy: Hands-on exposure to modern analytics platforms such as BigQuery, Looker, Tableau, or Connected Sheets.
Collaborative Delivery: Experience working alongside Data or Analytics Engineering teams to drive product rollouts and increase data adoption across the business.




What you’ll experience

Hybrid Flexibility: Blend in-office engagement with work-from-home opportunities for a balanced work week.
A global business with endless career possibilities around every corner and across every discipline – with valuable exposure to a vast and exciting business network.
Team discounts across our range of Woolworths Group brands you know and love and a robust rewards program that celebrates and incentivises purpose-driven work.
12 weeks paid parental leave for primary caregivers plus paid superannuation for up to 12 months while the Team Member is on parental leave - eligible from the first day of employment




Everyone belongs at Woolworths Group 

As one of the largest employers in Australia and New Zealand, we aim to create a truly inclusive workplace where everyone feels that they belong, can be their best selves, and reach their full potential.

Diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging are key to realising our purpose of better together for a better tomorrow. We recognise the value our team’s diversity brings to our business, customers, and communities and that teams with diverse experiences and backgrounds enrich our group and are better able to innovate and solve problems. 

Woolworths Group is an inclusive, team-first company and we value all skills and experiences. If you meet some, but not all of the requirements, we encourage you to submit your application.

Analytics Engineer – Asset Performance

Department: Operations – Asset Management & Utilities
Start Date: ASAP
Duration: 5-Month Contract
Roster: 5:2 (8 hours per day, 40 hours per week)
Location: Perth CBD

About the Role

We are seeking an experienced Analytics Engineer – Asset Performance to support a leading mining operation in delivering high-quality performance and asset reporting solutions.
This role will focus on developing a comprehensive reporting suite for the Water Management team to automate monthly water balance reporting, along with performance, reliability, and continuous improvement reporting. The position will also support broader operational performance reporting across Rail and Heavy Mobile Equipment (HME).
Working within an engineering-led analytics team, you will leverage your expertise in Snowflake SQL and Power BI to design scalable data models and pipelines that enable advanced analytics and strategic decision-making. You will play a key role in building the analytical foundation that empowers engineering stakeholders to analyse, interpret, and act on operational data.
This role requires someone who can transform large, complex datasets into structured, reliable, and accessible information for reporting and visualisation. You will also collaborate closely with internal technology teams to ensure seamless data integration between front-end and back-end systems, delivering trusted insights across the business.

Key Responsibilities

Develop and maintain Snowflake SQL data models and pipelines to support asset performance analytics and monthly water management reporting
Design and build Power BI dashboards and reports for water balance, reliability, and continuous improvement processes
Support the Asset Performance team with ongoing development and maintenance of operational reporting
Drive continuous improvement in data quality through investigation, validation, and proactive issue resolution
Utilise GitHub for version control, code review, and collaborative development
Maintain robust documentation and governance across analytical systems
About You

Strong experience working with SQL Server and/or Snowflake
Proven expertise in Power BI development
Experience working with delay accounting data highly regarded
Familiarity with Water Management, Rail, or mining operations strongly preferred
Experience with Python or similar data tools is advantageous
Highly motivated, analytical, and eager to expand knowledge in asset performance and operational analytics
Benefits

Perth CBD office location
Opportunity to work on large-scale mining operations
Exposure to complex asset performance and operational datasets
Collaborative, engineering-led analytics environment
Professional development and skill growth in advanced data modelling and analytics
 


At Chandler Macleod, we are committed to fostering a diverse workforce where everyone is welcome. We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, women, neurodiverse, people living with a disability and the LGBTIQA+ community.

You can read more about our commitment to diversity and inclusion at https://www.chandlermacleod.com/diversity-and-inclusion.

Job Title: Financial Planning & Analysis Analyst | ACT Gov | $100.63 per Hour
Ad Text:
Highly sought after Government Organisation

Up to $100.63 per hour 

Until 08/05/2026

Our client is a large Government organisation who are seeking an experienced Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A) Analyst to join their team.



The Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A) Analyst is responsible for delivering high-quality financial forecasting, budgeting, modelling, operational analysis, and insights to support the organisation. This includes supporting the financial planning cycle, forward budgets, future budget bids, and strategic decision-making.



Key Responsibilities: 

Build and maintain detailed financial forecasts, including revenue, cost, margin, and cashflow projections across PTG services.

Support internal/external budgets, ACT Government budget processes and future budget bids.

Conduct financial and operational variance analysis to identify drivers of performance and inefficiency.

Analyse operational processes (workflow, unit cost, productivity, demand patterns) to identify efficiency opportunities.

Selection Criteria:

Demonstrated FP&A experience including forecasting, budgeting, variance analysis and modelling.

Experience analysing operational processes, unit costs, productivity and service performance to identify efficiency improvements.

Advanced modelling skills using Excel and tools such as Power BI, Adaptive Insights, Anaplan or similar..

Tertiary qualifications in accounting, finance, economics, analytics or similar. CA/CPA/CFA progress desirable.

On offer is a contract until 08/05/2026 at a highly sought after Government organisation, with a highly competitive hourly rate.


To send your CV for review, please use the APPLY button below.

WHO ARE WE?

Join Tecala — one of Australia’s most awarded and fastest-growing ICT managed services providers — and play a key role in shaping the commercial intelligence capability that underpins our next phase of growth.

We’re certified as a Great Place to Work (2023–2025), ranked in the top 10 of Australia’s Best Workplaces in Technology 2025, and named Australia’s #1 Managed Service Provider and #29 globally in the 2025 MSP 501.

At Tecala, we don’t just deliver technology — we drive measurable business outcomes. Our Automation, Data & AI (ADA) division is at the forefront of helping customers unlock commercial value through data, automation, and artificial intelligence — and this role sits at the centre of that mission.




ABOUT THE ROLE

The Head of Commercial Data Insights is a senior role within Tecala’s Automation, Data & AI (ADA) department, responsible for driving measurable value creation across our customer base.

This is not a traditional BI or reporting role. The mandate is clear: identify commercial leverage within customer organisations, translate insight into financial impact, and support execution that improves revenue, margin, cost efficiency, and EBITDA outcomes.

You will combine strong commercial finance acumen with a structured, hypothesis-led problem-solving mindset. AI-enabled analytics tools will be leveraged to accelerate insight generation and scenario modelling at scale — but technology is the enabler. Commercial judgement, clarity of thinking, and value creation are the priority.

Working closely with customer leadership teams, you will help position ADA as a commercially focused transformation partner — not just a technology provider.

You’ll thrive in this role if you’re energised by ambiguity, motivated by impact, and driven to turn data into dollars for customers.

Key responsibilities will include (but not limited to):

Identifying and quantifying commercial value creation opportunities within customer organisations, including margin improvement, revenue optimisation, pricing uplift, and cost reduction initiatives.

Analysing unit economics, gross margin drivers, pricing dynamics, and cost structures to uncover actionable commercial levers.

Developing clear business cases and ROI models that directly link analytical insight to measurable financial outcomes.

Framing ambiguous operational or commercial challenges into structured, hypothesis-led analytical approaches.

Proactively identifying and deploying AI-enabled analytics tools (e.g. Claude, Claude Code, ChatGPT, SQL, advanced analytics platforms) to accelerate data interrogation, modelling, and insight generation.

Staying current with advances in AI and analytics tooling, continuously refining how these tools are applied to improve speed, quality, and repeatability of analysis.

Working closely with customer executive and operational stakeholders to translate insight into practical, executable initiatives.

Building repeatable analytical frameworks and commercial playbooks that can be deployed across multiple customer engagements.

Supporting ADA solution offerings by embedding commercial value modelling into automation, data, and AI transformation programs.

Required skills and experience:

3–6 years’ experience in commercial finance, strategy, consulting, or analytically intensive commercial roles.

Strong commercial finance acumen, including deep understanding of gross margin mechanics, unit economics, pricing strategy, and cost drivers.

Demonstrated ability to translate analysis into financial impact through business case development, scenario modelling, and ROI analysis.

Strong interest in AI, data, and emerging analytics tools (e.g. Claude, Claude Code, ChatGPT, SQL, Excel, Power BI/Tableau, Python/R preferred) or similar platforms to enhance productivity and insight generation.

Proven stakeholder influence skills, with experience working alongside senior leaders to convert insight into action.

Highly developed written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to simplify complex concepts and drive alignment.

High intellectual curiosity, comfort with ambiguity, and a bias toward solving problems rather than reporting on them.

Bachelor’s degree in Business, Commerce, Economics, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related discipline.




WHAT WE CAN OFFER YOU

As part of our team, you’ll be able to learn and grow in a supportive and inclusive environment. Some of the benefits available to you include:

Paid Birthday Leave

Inclusive paid parental leave policy that supports all parents & carers

Health & Wellness perks including discounted gym membership

Access to ongoing Learning & Development opportunities, including self-paced learning through our learning management system

Paid certifications and remuneration incentives to get upskilled / certified to support your development

Access to Career Pathways so you can continue building your career within the business

Reward & Recognition Program where you can thank and reward your teammates

Hybrid and flexible working model

Employee discounts – Access discounted rates and offers from a variety of providers, including health insurance and IT Hardware

Employee Assistance Program (EAP)




Note: As an essential part of our hiring process, you will be required to undertake a National Criminal History Check.




HOW TO APPLY

If you’re excited about the opportunity to drive measurable commercial impact for customers as part of our Automation, Data & AI division, meet the must-haves and tick some maybes, then apply now.

Be sure to include a cover letter and CV to support your application.




No agencies please – we’ve got this one covered.




We foster an inclusive and diverse work environment where our people can be themselves. Every idea and perspective is valued so that our services reflect the companies we support. We welcome diversity in all its forms and encourage applications from suitably qualified candidates across the range of underrepresented communities.

Company description:



Sandfire is an Australian mining and exploration company listed on the Australian Securities Exchange (ASX: SFR). Sandfire has a strong track record of safe, consistent and profitable production and a clear strategy to grow into a globally diversified and sustainable mining company.

Please see our social media house rules for further information on our approach to social media management: https://www.sandfire.com.au/about-sandfire/sandfire-social-media-house-rules/



Job description:



Sandfire continues to make strong progress toward its goal of being a sustainable mining company and global copper producer of significance and is listed on the Australian Securities Exchange (ASX: SFR).



Headquartered in Perth, Western Australia, Sandfire has a demonstrated commitment to the highest safety standards. Our strategy is focused on both near-mine and regional opportunities as we seek to leverage our strategic position in the Iberian Pyrite Belt in Spain and the Kalahari Copper Belt in Botswana and materially increase the life of our modern processing hubs.


Sandfire Resources has entered an exciting partnership with Havilah Resources to advance the Kalkaroo Copper-Gold Project in South Australia and commenced its prefeasibility study phase.

About the role

We are looking for an experienced IT Business Systems Analyst with a strong finance and ERP background to join our collaborative IT team, primarily supporting the corporate functions, as well as engagement with our operating assets where required. This role is key to bridging business and technology - enhancing systems and processes, streamlining data access, and enabling strategic decision-making.

You will contribute to supporting our key finance systems (Pronto Xi, Anaplan) while also working with other business stakeholders across multiple departments. Your finance experience will extend to analysis and reporting in support of our Data and Analytics team.

This role will also support a variety of other projects within the corporate function in Australia.

Responsibilities (Include but are not limited to)

Analyse and model existing business processes, identify areas for improvement, and support solution design.
Engage with business stakeholders to understand challenges, gather requirements, and define solution objectives.
Manage and prioritise enhancement requests and change controls related to business systems
Extract and analyse information from Pronto ERP
Document and translate business requirements into specifications for IT teams and solution partners.

About You

5+ years of experience in a similar role
Strong understanding of ERP systems (e.g., Pronto, SAP), including process analysis, configuration support, and stakeholder engagement.
Strong SQL skills
Proven experience as a Business Analyst within IT environments, preferably in the mining, resources, or industrial sectors.
Exposure to business process mapping (e.g., BPMN) and requirements gathering techniques.

At Sandfire we value our people and offer a range of benefits and personal development opportunities including:





Attractive, market-competitive remuneration package
Flexible work arrangements
Salary continuance insurance scheme.
Corporate health insurance discount.
Ongoing professional development opportunities





If you possess the above experience and would like to be part of a values-driven, inclusive, and respectful organisation, please apply now by clicking on the apply now button below.

Kindly note that agency applications will not be accepted for this position

About Us 

Welcome to the world of Lindt - where our rich and diverse culture embraces change while cherishing our history. At Lindt, we believe that excellence lies within each and every one of our employees. That's why we've created a one-of-a-kind environment where our team members can take risks, challenge the norm, and help build the Lindt brand for generations to come. 

With teams of passionate employees, we're always on the lookout for fresh ideas and innovative thinking. We encourage our team to recognise opportunities, get creative, and build strong relationships to Enchant the World with Chocolate. 


About the Role 

As a Senior BI Consultant, you will be a key member of the Lindt IT team, reporting to the Solutions Delivery Manager. This is a hands-on leadership role where you will drive the analysis, design, development, and delivery of SAP BI solutions across the APAC region. This position includes platform support, monitoring, end user support, training, and documentation for deployed BI solutions. We need someone who is intellectually curious, picks things up fast, takes ownership, and can lead by example while staying deeply hands-on. 

Key Responsibilities 

Lead end-to-end design, development, and delivery of SAP BI solutions across BW/4HANA, HANA modelling, and SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), covering Retail, Supply Chain, and Finance domains. 
Run requirements workshops with business stakeholders, translate needs into technical specs, and act as a trusted advisor on BI capabilities and data solutions. 
Architect and optimise data models, ETL processes, and reporting frameworks — mentoring junior team members on best practices along the way. 
Own comprehensive documentation across the full delivery lifecycle: design specs, data dictionaries, user guides, and training materials. 
Provide L2/L3 support, platform monitoring, and performance tuning to keep BI operations running smoothly.  

What You Will Bring 

At least 7+ years of hands-on experience in SAP BI/BW, with strong proficiency in SAP BW/4HANA, BW on HANA 
Expertise in HANA modelling (Calculation Views, Analytical Views, Procedures) using XSA or Web IDE. 
Extensive experience with ABAP/AMDP development for BI use cases. 
Proven ability to gather business requirements through workshops and stakeholder engagement – translating them into actionable technical solutions. 
Experience designing and delivering end-to-end BI solutions across complex business domains (Retail, Supply Chain, Finance). 
Proficiency in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) including dashboard development, stories, and data visualisations. 
Experience with Analysis for Office (AFO) for advanced Excel-based reporting. 
Ability to produce high-quality technical specifications, design documents, and user guides. 
Excellent troubleshooting and problem-solving skills with a proactive, solutions-oriented mindset.
Exceptional verbal communication skills, able to liaise with technical and non-technical stakeholders. 

It would be desirable if you also bring:

Familiarity with Power BI or other non-SAP BI tools and how they integrate with SAP data sources. 
Experience with SAP Data Intelligence or SAP Datasphere.

What We Can Offer You 

A positive, supportive environment that encourages both career and personal growth, celebrates achievements, and recognises success 
An engaging and creative workplace filled with passionate individuals who value diversity and think differently 
A hybrid working environment - 3 days in the office, 2 days working from home 
A strong focus on maintaining a healthy work life balance 
A paid day off on your birthday 

And yes there are chocolate perks, including the opportunity to enjoy and indulge in some of the finest chocolate in the world!

When you join UGL, you will collaborate with some of the Australias most talented experts to help shape the nation. With opportunities to work on large-scale projects from start to finish, you will have the space to grow your skills, pursue your passion, and be supported in creating a career journey that aligns with your aspirations and lifestyle.

UGL Utilities Operations & Maintenance business is a trusted provider of essential services across power, water, and gas sectors. With a strong reputation and long-standing contracts in power distribution, transmission, and water operations and maintenance, we are poised for significant growth.


About the role

The Systems & Improvement Lead plays a key role in ensuring operational efficiency across UGL Services by supporting, enhancing, and deploying digital and business systems. The role gathers stakeholder requirements, documents processes, manages system rollout projects, and provides ongoing support to operational teams. It contributes to continuous improvement initiatives that enable safer, better, faster, and more efficient ways of working across contracts.

This position will be based at our North Sydney office.

Key responsibilities

Gather, analyse, and translate stakeholder requirements into actionable system or process designs.

Document business processes and system workflows using clear process maps.

Maintain accurate and current documentation for operational systems.

Project manage the rollout of digital systems, including planning, design, development, testing, deployment,and handover to operations.

Work collaboratively with IT, Operations, and program stakeholders to ensure successful delivery within agreed timelines and budget constraints.

Support change management activities, including communications, training, and adoption planning.

Provide user support, troubleshooting, and system guidance to operational teams.

Coordinate updates, enhancements, and configuration changes in line with business needs.

Monitor system performance and proactively identify improvement opportunities.

Identify and document improvement opportunities across systems, workflows, and operational processes.

Support teams in applying improved ways of working that deliver measurable operational value.

Contribute to national or contract-level improvement initiatives as required.

Lead or support system related components of new contract mobilisations, ensuring readiness of digital tools, data, and processes.

Liaise with internal and external stakeholders to ensure smooth transition into operations.

About You

Minimum 5 years in a systems, process improvement, or similar role

Experience within construction, utilities, services, or project‑based environments

Proficiency with MS Office, SharePoint, Teams, Power BI, Power Apps, Power Automate, and Visio.

Experience with SAP, SAP Field Service Management (FSM), and Cognito Forms (desirable).

About UGL

As a member of the CIMIC Group of companies, we value diversity in our team’s backgrounds, ideas, work styles.

We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and strongly encourage applications from women, people with disabilities, Aboriginal & Torres Strait Islander people, mature aged workers and individuals from culturally diverse backgrounds.

What we offer

Enjoy a hybrid work arrangement working from home 1-2 days per week.

Benefit from 16 weeks of paid primary parental leave.

Complimentary salary continuance insurance.

Discounted Medibank health insurance.

Shape your career with an Individual Development Plan.

Participate in our Wellness Program with AIA Vitality.

Access our OneRewards Discount Program.

How to apply 

If this role sounds like you, please click on the apply button and follow the prompts. We will get in touch if you are shortlisted for this opportunity.

Please note: As part of the recruitment process, relevant screening checks will be conducted. These may include reference checks, verification of the right to work in Australia, a national criminal history check, anti-money laundering checks, and a pre-employment medical assessment (including drug and alcohol screening).

Job Ref 266396

We’re looking for a dedicated PowerBI Developer to join a major data transformation program, migrating from a legacy SQL/VM environment into Microsoft Fabric. This is a high-impact role within the BI team of the Data Architecture & Engineering function, focused on building out ~40 PowerBI models and reports that will shape the future of business intelligence across the organisation.

What you’ll be doing:
Designing and developing semantic models and reusable data layers in PowerBI.
Writing advanced DAX and applying dimensional modelling techniques.
Collaborating with stakeholders to refine requirements and ensure business logic is accurately represented.
Analysing legacy SAP Business Objects / Web Intelligence reports to support migration efforts.
Balancing independent development with stakeholder workshops (~50/50 split).
Working closely with the BI Lead and broader data/analytics teams to deliver fit-for-purpose reporting solutions.
What you’ll bring:
5+ years’ experience as a dedicated PowerBI Developer (not just a data analyst who uses PowerBI).
Strong skills in DAX, semantic model design, and dimensional modelling.
Experience in reporting transformation or migration projects.
Solid SQL skills and the ability to translate business requirements into technical specifications.
Exposure to SAP Business Objects or similar BI tools (highly desirable).
Excellent communication, stakeholder engagement, and self-management skills.
What’s in it for you?
Kick off your week with a career move that values your expertise.

Full-time (1.0 FTE), 12-month fixed-term contract
Competitive salary: $135,000–$150,000 + Super
Work on a high-impact transformation program with real business value
Gain hands-on experience with Microsoft Fabric, Azure ML, and advanced analytics tools
Enjoy flexibility with a Brisbane-based role and WFH options
Be part of a collaborative, remote-friendly team with scope to grow into broader data engineering and data science

The Company…..
Our client is an ACT Government directorate committed to delivering responsive, high-quality services across the Territory while supporting inclusive and sustainable community outcomes. Each division is led by experienced professionals who work collaboratively to strengthen programs, enhance service delivery, and drive continuous improvement across government initiatives.
The successful candidate will bring demonstrated experience providing financial insights and strategic advice to senior stakeholders within a complex public sector environment.

This role is based in Canberra, with flexible hybrid working arrangements available. An immediate start is required for an initial three-month engagement, with the potential for extension.

 
The Job…..
Reporting to the Finance Manager in this FP&A Analyst role, you will be responsible for:

Build and maintain financial forecasts, including revenue, costs, margins, and cash flow.
Support budgeting processes and future budget proposals.
Conduct financial and operational variance analysis to identify performance drivers and inefficiencies.

Catholic Education in Western Australia (CEWA) is the second largest education provider in the State. We cater for more than 76,000 students in 162 schools.

CEWA strives to create progressive environments that enhance learning. Our culture is underpinned by our Vision, Quality Catholic Education (QCE) Elements and CECWA' Strategic Directions. Our aspiration is to build Catholic Schools of Excellence, underpinned by their evangelising Mission, in a system where all students, staff and parents are empowered as active members of their school and parish communities.





Senior Data Engineer

For over 150 years, our schools, colleges, early learning centres and out of school care programs, have been an integral part of education in Western Australia. Together we form the state's second-largest education provider, with more than 83,000 students relying on our nearly 14,000 dedicated staff each day. We are committed to fostering an environment where every child can achieve their full potential, and our office teams support schools to do exactly that.

The Role

The role of Senior Data Engineer belongs to the Application Development team in the Finance, Infrastructure and Digital Technology directorate. We have 5 office campuses across the state and this role will be based at our West Leederville office.

We are seeking 2 full-time ongoing Senior Data Engineers commencing in late March 2026.

The Senior Data Engineer is responsible for technical leadership of cloud data platforms, leading the design and build of strategic data products, as well as leading internal consultancy activities to enable business users and analysts to obtain valuable data insights.

The Primary accountabilities of this role include but and not limited to:

Provide leadership in designing, building and maintaining existing ETL frameworks, cloud data platform components, working with Azure SQL, Databricks and Azure Data Factory.
Lead the build and maintenance of strategic data products which conform to CEWA's business and non-functional requirements, using Power BI and other front-end technologies.
Ensure Human-Centered Design tools & skills are used to ensure effective UX and high-quality UI in data products, using Figma and other design tools.
Lead various internal consultancy services to business unit-based Business Intelligence Analyst, Business Analysts and other business users to facilitate data access, data literacy, understanding of technical tools and processes, to support business self-service.
Lead the design, implementation and maintenance of dimensional data models using Kimball methodologies (Star Schema) to ensure high performance and consistency for analytics products.
Perform complex data analysis and reporting using SQL, DAX, Python, Power BI and other technologies.
Ensure automated tests are implemented as part of all data product builds.
Provide leadership in utilising appropriate AI and ML tools and processes during the SDLC and within product features.
Ensure the team delivers a suitable level of documentation, including Data Definitions, Product & Process Descriptions, Support Wikis, etc.
Ensure a high Cybersecurity focus in all stages of the SDLC.

Benefits of the role

A competitive salary: Level L on the CEWA salary scale [$169,821 - $192,025 per annum] plus superannuation.
Additional paid leave during office closures over Christmas and Easter.
Salary packaging options in line with our not-for-profit status, including novated leasing.
Deferred salary program for eligible employees after two years of service.
Free onsite parking.
Corporate health insurance discounts.
Access to the Fitness Passport program.
Employee Assistance Program: access confidential counselling and wellbeing support for yourself and your family.
Ongoing professional learning opportunities, including a CEWA scholarship program to support further education.
Flexible work arrangements, where operationally appropriate.
Wellbeing initiatives including annual flu vaccinations and workplace health checks.
Parental leave provisions above statutory minimums.

Selection Criteria

A. ESSENTIAL:

Prepared to support the Catholic ethos in the performance of their duties.
Experience in a technical leadership role, in a collaborative and trust-based team environment.
7+ years of experience working as a Data Engineer or Business Intelligent Specialist, with Databricks or equivalent technologies.
7+ years developing Business Intelligence reports and dashboards in Power BI.
Strong programming skills and technical experience for data manipulation and management using PySpark, T/SQL and DAX or equivalent.
Strong understanding of data modelling patterns.
Strong UX and UI skills with proven ability to harness the full functionality of Power BI.
Experience optimising Azure/Power BI data solutions for performance.

Ad Text:
Your new role:

As the Senior Director - Health Funding Analysis, you will lead Metro South Health's strategic health funding and activity function, reporting directly to the Chief Finance and Sustainability Officer. You will provide executive leadership, expert advice and governance oversight to optimise activity-based funding (ABF) outcomes, strengthen funding integrity, and support financial sustainability. Within this role you will:

Provide enterprise-wide leadership on ABF, funding reforms, and State and Commonwealth policy, supporting service planning and financial strategy.
Analyse Weighted Activity Units, case-mix complexity, cost structures, and funding variances to drive performance improvement and support executive decision-making.
Advise on major projects, service redesign, and hospital expansion, modelling funding impacts and risks under multiple scenarios.
Assist in negotiations with the Department of Health on funding arrangements, activity targets, and performance issues, advocating for Metro South Health.
Represent the Health Service in state and national ABF forums, committees, and working groups, contributing to funding policy, classification, and costing standards.
Lead, develop, and sustain a multidisciplinary team of ABF analysts, Health Information Managers, and clinicians, building capability in funding intelligence and performance management.
About you:

You are a senior financial leader with a strategic mindset and a proven track record in managing complex funding, ABF, and performance frameworks. You excel at translating complex technical and analytical information into actionable insights and have strong experience guiding executive decision-making. Your leadership fosters collaboration, capability building, and accountability. In addition, you have:

Extensive experience in ABF, health funding strategy, financial governance, and performance management within a large, public sector organisation or multi-facility health service.
Proven ability to lead and inspire high-performing, multidisciplinary teams and manage complex stakeholder relationships.
Exceptional analytical, strategic, and communication skills, with the ability to influence and negotiate at an executive level.

For comprehensive information about this opportunity and what we are looking for in the ideal candidate, please view the attached role description.

What we can offer you:
We offer a family-friendly work environment that supports you in making the most of Queensland's laid-back lifestyle.
We are renowned for teaching and research excellence with strong relations with Queensland's leading tertiary institutions.
We want to empower you to build a long-term career, with countless opportunities to learn and grow, no matter your role
You'll have all the benefits of working for Queensland's world-class public health system including generous leave entitlements, attractive salary and remuneration packages, salary packaging and access to relevant professional development allowances and/or leave, study and research assistance, corporate discounts and employee wellness programs.
About us:

Having a workforce that reflects and understands the needs and expectations of our community is important to delivering safe, kinder, and more inclusive care.

We recognise our strength comes from the diversity of our people and so we encourage people of all genders, ethnicities, ages, abilities, languages, sexual orientation, and family responsibilities to apply.

INTEGRITY | COMPASSION | ACCOUNTABILITY | RESPECT | ENGAGEMENT | EXCELLENCE

We are bold, innovative, collaborative, inspiring. We save lives, change lives, and make the world a better place. We are part of and proud of the community we serve.

We are better together.

MinterEllison is one of Australia's largest independent law and consulting firms. With a heritage of almost 200 years, we are known for our excellence, and our authentic and enduring relationships with our clients, our people and our communities. Clients seek out MinterEllison to help them solve complex problems every day. We do this by our full-service legal offering and complementary consulting offering.

We offer opportunities to work on industry-leading mandates for top-tier clients, while being part of a high-performing and collaborative team that values excellence, diversity of thought, curiosity, and inclusion. We value our people and empower them to achieve their ambitions - with the support, trust and autonomy to grow their careers in meaningful ways.

At MinterEllison, we're leading the way with AI and other emerging technologies, powering innovation right across our firm. You will have the opportunity to engage with AI tools we've built, and other external AI tools, to enhance efficiencies and excellence internally and with our clients.

We support sustainable ways of working regarding how, when and where you work and offer a wide range of social, financial and health benefits (see https://www.minterellison.com/-/media/Minter-Ellison/Files/Careers/MinterEllison-Employee-Benefits-Dec_2025.pdf)

The Role

This role sits within the Business Insights and Solutions team in Financial Services and supports Finance-led initiatives through hands-on data analysis, validation and execution.

You will work across both project-based and operational activities, collaborating with Finance, Technology and Data teams to support data quality, system integration, testing and documentation. While reporting tools form part of the role, the primary focus is working with data across interconnected systems - understanding how it is created, transformed, controlled and consumed.

The role contributes practical delivery support to Finance transformation initiatives, including regulatory programs (such as AML), platform change and system refactoring. Success in this role is measured by delivery reliability, data integrity and knowledge continuity, rather than ownership of formal governance or strategic direction.

Key Responsibilities

Analyse and reconcile data across multiple enterprise systems using SQL, Python and Excel to investigate issues, validate outcomes and support Finance decisions.
Develop a strong understanding of finance platforms (including Elite) and how data structures support reporting and operational processes.
Work closely with Finance stakeholders to clarify and structure data and process requirements that enable effective solution design and delivery.
Provide data-driven impact analysis to support Finance initiatives, reviewing workflows and data flows to identify inefficiencies, control risks and upstream and downstream impacts across reporting, finance operations, internal controls and connected systems. This includes cross-system data mapping and integration work (e.g. expense management, data warehouses and other finance platforms).
Support major transformation initiatives, including regulatory programs (such as AML) and Elite on-prem to cloud migration, assisting with impact assessment, testing, reporting validation and staged or interim solutions to ensure data integrity and continuity.
Support system testing and UAT activities, including preparation of validation checks, defect follow-up and maintenance of clear documentation (decision logs, data notes and process descriptions), contributing to knowledge transfer and cross-team coordination across Finance, Technology and Data teams.
Contribute to reducing key-person risk by capturing system knowledge, data structures and operational decision history in a structured and reusable manner.

About You

Strong experience working with data across multiple systems within complex organisational environments, with the ability to investigate, reconcile and clearly explain impacts
Proven analytical and problem-solving skills, comfortable operating in ambiguity and evolving priorities
Experience supporting Finance or operational system initiatives, with awareness of data integrity, controls and compliance considerations
Strong SQL and advanced Excel capability; experience with scripting or automation tools (e.g. Python) is advantageous
Able to contribute to system improvement and refactoring discussions without acting as a Business Analyst or owning formal requirements
Strong documentation discipline and the ability to capture system knowledge clearly and accurately
Comfortable operating in an execution-focused role, contributing through hands-on analysis, coordination and follow-through

Qualifications & Experience

Bachelor's degree in Data Analytics, Information Systems, Business, or a related field
Experience working in complex enterprise environments; professional services experience preferred
Experience working with enterprise finance platforms (e.g. Elite 3E), particularly within a legal or matter-based environment, strongly preferred given the complexity of systems and platform initiatives underway
Experience supporting system migration, integration or transformation initiatives is advantageous
Experience with reporting or visualisation tools (including Power BI) useful, but this role is not primarily focused on dashboard development





How to apply

We encourage applications from people of all ages, abilities, cultural backgrounds, genders (including trans or gender diverse), LGBTQ+ people and those with carer responsibilities. We particularly encourage Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people to apply.

We prefer to connect with people directly, so please submit your CV by clicking on the 'Apply' button. We encourage all applications, including if you do not meet all the criteria listed for the role. Your application will also enable us to consider you for other opportunities that may be available at MinterEllison.

If you are currently a MinterEllison employee, please apply through the internal careers page.

If you would like further information, require any adjustments throughout the recruitment process or for a confidential discussion, please contact Danielle.Miller@minterellison.com.

The Commercial Manager, Personal Loans AU/NZ is responsible for driving sustainable growth and profitability of the Personal Loans business. This role operates as a key member of the Product and Commercial team, and sits at the intersection of strategy, analytics, and execution translating data-driven insights into commercial outcomes across pricing, product, distribution, and performance management.

The role partners closely with Credit, Risk, Finance, Marketing, and Distribution to optimise growth while maintaining disciplined returns.

This role is responsible for supporting and executing the product and portfolio strategy across Personal Loans and Motor Loans, with a focus on delivering sustainable revenue growth, strong customer outcomes and disciplined risk management. The role contributes across the end-to-end product lifecycle, including pricing, portfolio performance management, analysis, and continuous improvement. The role helps balance growth objectives, ensuring the portfolios remain competitive, scalable and aligned to future market needs.

What will you contribute to?

Commercial Strategy & Growth

Develop and execute commercial strategies to drive profitable growth across the Personal Loans & Auto portfolio
Identify and evaluate growth opportunities across customer segments, channels, and products
Support new product initiatives, enhancements, and market expansion opportunities, in conjunction with the Product Management team.
Optimise existing customer strategies across retention, redraw, and top up transactions.

Analytics & Performance Management

Optimise and champion key commercial metrics including volume, margin, yield, RAI
Analyse portfolio performance including detailed vintage analysis, customer behaviour, and channel economics to inform decision-making.
Build and maintain forecasting models, business cases, and scenario analysis
Monitor performance against targets and recommend corrective actions where required

Pricing & Product Economics

Support pricing strategies that balance growth, competitiveness, and risk-adjusted returns
Assess the impact of pricing, policy, and product changes on portfolio performance
Work with Risk and Credit teams to ensure commercial objectives align with risk appetite

Stakeholder Management

Partner with internal stakeholders across Risk, Credit, Finance, Operations, and Marketing
Provide clear, data-driven insights and recommendations to senior leadership
Lead and coordinate key performance forum with high-quality reporting and commercial analysis.

Continuous Improvement

Identify process, reporting, and analytical improvements to enhance decision-making
Contribute to a culture of commercial discipline and performance accountability to ensure initiatives deliver intended commercial and customer outcomes.
Demonstrate new ways of working and enhance capability through AI.

What you’ll bring: 

Experience

5–7+ years’ experience in commercial, analytical, or strategy roles within financial services
Experience in personal loans, consumer finance, banking, or fintech strongly preferred
Demonstrated track record of driving growth through data-led insights
AI-native - regularly leverages AI tools to enhance decision-making, automate reporting, accelerate content creation, and drive greater efficiency across your team

Technical & Analytical Capability

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to translate data into clear commercial actions
Advanced Excel / financial modelling capability; experience with BI tools highly regarded
Comfortable working with large datasets and performance metrics

Commercial Acumen

Strong understanding of unit economics, margins, and portfolio performance
Ability to balance growth ambition with disciplined returns
Experience influencing outcomes without direct authority

Personal Attributes

Commercially minded and outcomes-focused resulting in measurable improvements.
Confident communicator, able to present insights clearly to senior stakeholders
Proactive, curious, and comfortable operating in a dynamic environment
Strong strategic mindset – able to see the bigger picture and integrate customer, risk, financial and operational perspectives.
Ability to roll sleeves up and dive headfirst into analysis and requests.

Sometimes the best candidates don’t have 100% of what is listed above, but if you have most and are confident, you’d be a good fit, we’d love to hear from you!

Shape the Future of Agribusiness | Drive Data-Led Decisions | Make Regional Impact

 

About Us

We are a well-established lamb and beef processing business located in Bunbury, Western Australia, servicing both domestic and international markets. With deep roots in the regional agricultural economy, our operation is built on operational excellence, sustainability, and end-to-end value-chain optimisation.

Our success is driven by strong partnerships with producers, customers, and the wider supply chain. We are committed to making informed, ethical, and commercially sound decisions that support long-term growth for our business and the communities we operate in.

 

Our Values

INTEGRITY – We do what’s right, even when it’s complex
COLLABORATION – Strong outcomes are built together
CURIOSITY – We seek better ways to operate and improve
ACCOUNTABILITY – We own our decisions and results

 

About the Role

We are seeking a Senior Agribusiness Analyst to join our leadership-facing commercial team at our Bunbury lamb and beef processing facility.

Reporting to the Finance Manager, this influential role partners closely with executive leadership and operations to deliver high-quality commercial insight across the entire red meat value chain.

This is a senior, hands-on role that blends strategic analysis, financial modelling, and operational insight to support profitability, growth, and risk management across livestock procurement, processing, and sales.

 

Key Responsibilities

Lead financial, operational, and market analysis across livestock procurement, processing efficiency, yields, and product margins
Develop and maintain advanced financial models covering pricing, throughput, cost structures, and capital investment scenarios
Analyse lamb and beef market trends, seasonal supply dynamics, and pricing volatility
Partner with operations to identify productivity improvements, yield optimisation, and cost-reduction opportunities
Provide commercial input into budgeting, forecasting, and long-range strategic planning
Support commercial negotiations with producers, agents, and customers through robust data-driven insights
Prepare high-quality reports, dashboards, and presentations for senior leadership and the board
Mentor junior analysts and contribute to continuous improvement of analytical capability, systems, and tools

 

Skills & Experience

Tertiary qualifications in Agribusiness, Finance, Economics, Accounting, or a related discipline
7–10+ years’ experience in agribusiness, food processing, commodities, or a similar commercial environment
Strong understanding of the red meat supply chain (lamb and beef highly desirable)
Advanced financial modelling, forecasting, and data analysis capability
High proficiency in Excel and financial systems; experience with ERP, BI tools, or SQL highly regarded
Strong commercial acumen with the ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights
Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills, including at executive level

 

Why Join Us

Senior, influential role within a critical regional agribusiness
Competitive remuneration package commensurate with experience
Opportunity to work closely with executive leadership and shape commercial strategy
Relocation support may be considered for the right candidate
Lifestyle benefits of living and working in the Bunbury region

Collaborate closely with engineering, architecture, compliance, and business teams.

About You:

Proven experience working on data foundations, not just dashboards or isolated models.

Ability to balance innovation with control, documentation, and traceability.

Strong analytical thinking with the ability to explain complex concepts simply.

Collaborative mindset and ability to work closely with senior engineers, architects, and business stakeholders.

Understanding of CI/CD pipeline experience.
